headline: Stocks Set to Open Lower as Oil Rises Amid Iran Impasse, Nvidia Earnings and Fed Minutes Awaited

headline: Global centres in India slow hiring as AI reshapes work, ANSR CEO says
summary: Global capability centres in India are being measured while hiring as companies are wary ‌about the impact of geopolitical uncertainties and growing AI adoption, the ‌CEO of ANSR, which helps firms build and run global centres, said on Monday.  India is home ​to more than half of the world's global centres as companies prefer its large skilled workforce, lower operating costs and rising ability to support high-value jobs across technology, finance and engineering.  However, the rise of artificial intelligence could test that edge by ‌reducing headcounts for some roles ⁠and reshaping the kind of work global centres do.

raw.headline: Global centres in India slow hiring as AI reshapes work, ANSR CEO says

India is home ​to more than half of the world's global centres as companies prefer its large skilled workforce, lower operating costs and rising ability to support high-value jobs across technology, finance and engineering.  However, the rise of artificial intelligence could test that edge by ‌reducing headcounts for some roles ⁠and reshaping the kind of work global centres do.
